Get Started

AI Implementation Consulting: From "We Should Use AI" to Actually Using It

Most companies bought AI tools in 2024 and 2025. Most of them aren't getting value from them. An AI implementation consultant closes the gap between the subscription and the result.

Talk to an AI Implementation Consultant → Start with an AI Audit
The Real Problem

The gap between buying AI tools
and getting value from them is large.

It's not a technology problem. The tools work. The gap is workflow design, configuration, training, and the organizational change required to make AI genuinely useful rather than occasionally convenient.

~70%
of companies with AI subscriptions report "limited" or "no" meaningful ROI
3–6mo
typical lag between AI tool purchase and first consistent team usage
1 in 5
enterprise AI initiatives produce measurable productivity gains within the first year
What goes wrong without an implementation specialist

Teams get access to AI tools and use them ad hoc, individually, without shared context or standards. Output quality varies wildly. No one maintains the configuration. Prompts are written from scratch for every task. The "AI initiative" gradually gets used by three enthusiasts and ignored by everyone else. The subscription renews, the ROI doesn't appear, and leadership concludes that AI isn't ready for their business — when the real problem is that the implementation was never done.

Common Problem 1
Individuals Using AI, Teams Not
One or two people in the company use Claude or ChatGPT productively. Everyone else finds it inconsistent and gives up. The problem is shared context and standards — not individual capability. Implementation creates the infrastructure that makes AI work at the team level.
Common Problem 2
Tools Purchased, Workflows Unchanged
The company bought AI tools and bolted them onto existing processes. The gains are marginal because the workflow was designed for a pre-AI world. Real ROI comes from redesigning workflows around what AI makes possible — not from adding AI to the workflow you already have.
Common Problem 3
Executives Under Pressure to Show ROI
The board or CEO has asked for an "AI strategy." The leadership team has been tasked with showing results. Without someone who knows how to structure an implementation that produces measurable outcomes, the initiative stalls in pilots and proof-of-concept that never scale.
What the Engagement Covers

Five phases of AI implementation —
from audit to ongoing optimization.

This is not a training course or a workshop. It's a full implementation — the audit, the setup, the training, and the ongoing work to make sure the system keeps improving after we're done with the initial build.

01
Workflow Audit
We map your current workflows across the functions you want to improve — typically marketing, sales, operations, or customer service — and identify the highest-leverage AI integration points. Not all tasks benefit equally from AI. The audit tells you where to invest time and configuration effort for maximum return. Output: a prioritized implementation roadmap with expected impact and effort estimates for each initiative.
02
Tool Selection & Stack Design
We assess your current AI subscriptions and identify whether you have the right tools for the workflows you're trying to improve. Many companies have overlapping subscriptions, gaps in critical areas, or tools that are poorly matched to their actual use cases. We make specific recommendations — including what to drop — and design the minimal stack that covers your actual needs. We're not affiliated with any AI vendors; our recommendations are based on fit, not commission.
03
Claude Setup & Configuration
For most business teams, Claude is the right primary AI tool. We build your Claude Projects from the ground up — system prompts that reflect your actual business, knowledge files that give Claude the context it needs to produce consistent output, and usage norms that ensure quality. We typically build 3–5 dedicated Projects in the first engagement: one per functional area, each configured to the specific work that team does.
04
Team Training & Adoption
Implementation fails when the tools are configured but the team doesn't know how to use them. We run structured training sessions with each functional team — not generic "how to use AI" training, but specific, role-based instruction using the actual Projects and prompts we've built for them. We cover what each tool is for, how to get good output, what to review before using output, and what to do when something doesn't work. We also establish feedback channels so issues surface quickly and are fixed.
05
Ongoing Optimization
The first version of any AI implementation is not the best version. After the initial build, we conduct monthly reviews: what's working, what isn't, what needs to be updated as your business changes, and where there are new opportunities to apply AI to workflows that weren't in scope initially. Businesses that treat AI implementation as a one-time project get one-time results. Businesses that maintain and iterate on their AI systems see compounding improvement.
Who Needs This

Three types of companies that need
an AI implementation consultant.

⚠️
Companies with AI Tools That Aren't Working
You have Claude, ChatGPT, or other AI subscriptions. Usage is scattered and inconsistent. Different team members use it differently, with wildly different results. Nobody is maintaining the configuration. The subscription cost is justified on the hope that AI is strategic, but you can't point to concrete ROI. You need someone to build the structure that makes the tools actually work — not for one person, but for the team.
🏗️
Teams That Want to Start Right
You haven't committed to a set of AI tools yet, or you're expanding from individual use to team-wide use. You want to build the system correctly from the beginning rather than accumulating technical debt from ad hoc adoption. You understand that the cost of getting this right upfront is far lower than the cost of undoing a poorly designed implementation six months from now.
📊
Executives Under Pressure to Show AI ROI
You've been asked — by your board, your CEO, your investors — to demonstrate that your AI investment is producing results. You need an implementation that produces measurable outcomes: time saved, output increased, cost reduced. Not a pilot project, not a workshop, not a proof-of-concept. An implementation with before-and-after metrics that show the board what you've actually built.
What You Get

Outcomes from a complete AI
implementation engagement.

Before Implementation
Individual team members using AI inconsistently
No shared context — Claude starts fresh in every conversation
Output quality varies by who's prompting and how
AI tools treating as individual productivity apps
No measurement of AI-related time savings or output gains
New hires have no AI workflow to onboard to
Subscriptions justified by potential, not results
After Implementation
Team-wide Claude Projects with shared context, consistent output
Prompt library of 20–40 tested prompts for your most common tasks
Workflow redesigns that cut admin time and increase output volume
Quality standards and review processes that maintain output integrity
Baseline metrics and ongoing measurement of actual ROI
Onboarding documentation so new hires adopt the AI workflow immediately
Monthly optimization reviews to keep the system improving
Related Services

AI implementation sits inside
a broader set of services.

Depending on what you need, implementation may be one component of a larger engagement — or it may be exactly the focused project you're looking for.

Start Here
AI Audit
Not sure where to start? The AI audit maps your current state, identifies the highest-leverage opportunities, and produces a prioritized roadmap before any implementation work begins.
Focused Build
Implementation Service
The full implementation — audit, tool selection, Claude setup, training, and ongoing optimization. For companies ready to move from experiment to infrastructure.
Small Business
Claude for Small Business
A scaled version of the implementation designed for companies with under 25 employees. Faster, more focused, and priced for smaller teams.
Strategic Layer
AI Strategy Consulting
For companies that need help with the strategic layer — what AI should and shouldn't do, how to structure the internal capability, and how to communicate the AI roadmap to the board.
AI Audit Details → Implementation Service → Claude for Small Business → AI Strategy Consulting →

that aren't producing results?
